separationRule: Separate CPD menu/workflow; do not merge into calculators/toolboxes.
cpdCentralFindings: Advertises accredited courses on a simple platform, learn on your schedule
Built-environment workshops/courses in Southern Africa, including SACAP-facing content
Architectural CPD administration/certificate submission service shown at R380/year or R59.99/month
Engineer CPD administration advertised under CESA accreditation with ECSA annual submission at R535/year
Digital resource bank includes municipal, SACAP and SANS resources
guardrails: AI only drafts assessments; human review required
Accredited credit values must be provider/reviewer approved
Learners cannot self-award CPD credits
Server/admin-controlled scoring and certificate issuance
No fake SACAP/ECSA sync when no real API/provider exists
Respect source content permissions and POPIA
No fake integration with any professional body unless official API/permission exists; use connector modes instead
Certificates auto-issue only after accredited live course, passing server-side score, and no pending manual review
Paid assessments require confirmed payment/free entitlement before assessment runner starts
Assessment price, platform fee percent, minimum fee and payout settings must be admin-controlled and audit logged
Category and credit value must be approved/confirmed by the relevant professional body, VA, CPD Central or accrediting partner before certificate issue
Architecture/SACAP 0.1 credit per hour must not be assumed for SACPLAN/ECSA/SACQSP/SACLAP/SACPCMP/SAGC without body-specific confirmation
Do not apply SACAP architecture credit rules to engineers, quantity surveyors, construction managers, planners, landscape architects, geomatics practitioners or valuers.
Onboarding must collect professional role, professional body and registration category before the CPD engine calculates credits/points/hours.
Certificates must use the body-specific unit label and category wording: credits, points, hours, or compliance status.
Where body rules are incomplete, degrade to manual verification and do not invent CPD values.

monetizationRequirements.principle: Content creators/lecturers must be able to monetize CPD assessment access at low per-assessment prices while Architex takes a configurable platform fee.
guardrails: No assessment start unless free or paid purchase is confirmed
Learners cannot alter price or fee records
Platform fee must be admin-configurable
Content-owner payout is separate from CPD accreditation approval
Pricing must distinguish post-webinar add-on assessments, standalone article-based assessments, and dedicated CPD course assessments

pricingModels.paid_webinar_addon_assessment: Lower add-on assessment fee when the learner already paid a higher-value webinar/course fee through CPD Central or another partner; fee covers extra assessed CPD evidence/credits.
pricingModels.standalone_article_based_assessment: Higher-value assessment price where the uploaded/approved article or reading is consumed primarily through Architex and the assessment/evidence is the monetised CPD product.
pricingModels.dedicated_cpd_course_assessment: Price set by judgement/admin approval based on expected assessment time, approved CPD category, approved credit value, content depth and content-owner proposal.
pricingModels.partner_bundle_included: Assessment included where partner contract already bundles course and assessment fees.
pricingModels.free_launch_or_partner_funnel: Free where used as launch marketing, sponsorship, or strategic partner funnel.

cpdCategoryResearch.strategicPrinciple: Architex CPD should be framed around professional-body CPD categories, with Category 1 / developmental learning as the strategic target where the accrediting body/CPD Central/VA can approve it.

SACAP.category1Rule: Generally 10 hours = 1 credit, i.e. 0.1 credit/hour, except mentoring rules. At least 1 Category 1 credit is compulsory annually, with 5 Category 1 credits required over the 5-year cycle. Up to 5 Category 1 credits may be accrued in one calendar year and claimed for the full cycle.
SACAP.platformImplication: Course/assessment should store requested and approved SACAP Category 1 credits separately from raw duration. A 1-hour SACAP Category 1 activity estimates to 0.1 credit unless accreditor-approved otherwise.

SACPLAN.ruleSummary: SACPLAN CPD policy uses 75 points over a 3-year cycle, with Category A Professional Knowledge, Category B Mentorship and Category C Active Participation. Some non-credit-bearing planning-related courses are 1 point per hour subject to sub-area limits.
SACPLAN.platformImplication: Do not force SACPLAN into SACAP credits. Store body-specific unit label as points.

ECSA.platformImplication: ECSA has CPD portals/documents/service-provider structures; do not issue ECSA category/credit claims until current ECSA or licensed-body approval confirms category and credits.

cpdCategoryResearch.guardrail: Never assume architecture 0.1 credit/hour applies across all built-environment bodies. Store professionalBody, approvedCategory, creditUnitLabel, requestedCredits, approvedCredits, ruleStatus and accreditor approval evidence.

SACAP.rule: 10 hours = 1 credit; 1 hour = 0.1 credit; minimum 1 Category 1 credit/year; 5 Category 1 credits/5-year cycle

ECSA.rule: 1 credit = 10 notional hours; minimum 5 Category 1a credits per cycle; minimum 3 credits/year across at least two categories

SACQSP.rule: Category 1 minimum 10 hours/year; Category 2 maximum 15 hours/year; Code of Conduct assessment pass mark 72%

SACPCMP.rule: Professional Practice 100; Personal Development 50; Mentorship 100; PPPI 50 over three financial years

SACPLAN.rule: Points model; selected planning courses may be 1 point/hour subject to focus-area rules

SACLAP.rule: Category 1: 1 hour = 0.1 credits; minimum 3 credits/year including 1 Category 1 credit/year; cycle target 25 or 20 depending registration category

SAGC.rule: Professionals/technologists 20 credits/cycle; technicians 13; minimum 5 Category 1/cycle; 1 ethics credit/cycle; Category 1 uses 10 hours = 1 credit

SACPVP.rule: Official public material confirms CPD compliance affects 5-year vs 1-year certificate validity; exact points/category formula requires manual confirmation
